basics.name: Vadim Nicolai
basics.label: Senior Full Stack Engineer
basics.summary: Senior Full Stack Engineer with 12+ years of experience building scalable web applications using React, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Expert in modern frontend architectures, microservices, GraphQL APIs, and performance-critical systems. Active open-source contributor to Nautilus Trader algorithmic trading platform with 100+ merged PRs in Rust and Python. Domain expertise in E-commerce, FinTech, AI/ML, and enterprise SaaS.
basics.keySkills: React • TypeScript • JavaScript • Next.js • Node.js • Go • Rust • GraphQL • Apollo • REST API • Microservices • gRPC • AWS • Cloudflare • MongoDB • DynamoDB • Docker • CI/CD • Jest • Git • AI/LLM Orchestration

work.name: Vitrifi
work.summary:
• Architected and delivered micro-frontend portal architecture using Go microservices with gRPC and OpenAPI, integrating shared React components across multiple teams to improve development velocity and code reusability.
  • Led API migration from OpenAPI REST to GraphQL, implementing Apollo Server in Go and Apollo Client in React, reducing data over-fetching and improving frontend performance.
  • Drove frontend architecture decisions and technical leadership across multiple teams, establishing consistent design patterns and enforcing code quality through comprehensive PR reviews.
  • Optimized monorepo infrastructure by migrating from npm to PNPM with Turborepo caching, reducing CI/CD build times and improving developer productivity.
  • Designed and implemented integration testing strategy using Jest and React Testing Library, establishing automated test pipelines in CI/CD.
  • Tech stack: Go, Echo, gRPC, GraphQL, Apollo Server, OpenAPI, React, TypeScript, Apollo Client, React Query, Ant Design, Turborepo, PNPM, MongoDB, Jest, React Testing Library

work.name: Wunderman Thompson Commerce
work.summary:
• Built high-performance e-commerce components for shopping cart and checkout flow using React and TypeScript.
  • Implemented SSR with Next.js for improved SEO and page load performance.

work.name: Event Espresso
work.summary:

  • Collaborated with product team to design user interfaces for event registration and ticketing workflows.
  • Tech stack: React 16, React Apollo, GraphQL, WPGraphQL, WordPress, PHP, MySQL, Jest, Playwright.

work.name: Modus Create, Inc
work.summary:
• Project: AARP Money Map - Built scalable financial planning platform for AARP, helping millions of users navigate financial decisions.
  • Designed component-based UI library with 40+ reusable React components in collaboration with UX designers, ensuring WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility.
  • Implemented responsive design patterns with CSS Grid and Flexbox for optimal cross-device experience.

  • Configured CI/CD pipeline with Bitbucket Pipelines, Sentry, SonarQube, and AWS CodePipeline for automated testing and deployments.

  • Tech stack: React 16.8, Hooks, Context API, React Apollo, Serverless Framework, GraphQL, DynamoDB, AWS Lambda, JavaScript ES6+

work.name: Atomate
work.summary:
• Project: Travlrr - Built reusable React component library using advanced patterns including Higher-Order Components (HOCs), render props, and compound components.
  • Implemented secure authentication system with AWS Lambda, Auth0, JWT tokens, and MongoDB.
  • Designed and built RESTful APIs using Node.js and Express.js following JSON:API specification.
  • Conducted code reviews and improved test coverage with Jest and Enzyme, reducing production bugs by 30%.
  • Optimized application performance through code splitting, lazy loading, and tree shaking, reducing initial bundle size by 45%.
  • Collaborated with UI/UX designers to implement pixel-perfect, responsive UI components with seamless mobile experience.
  • Tech stack: React 16, JavaScript ES6+, Node.js, Express.js, MongoDB, AWS Lambda, Auth0, Redux, Webpack

aiProjects.name: Nomadically.work
aiProjects.description: Full-stack AI job board aggregator for remote EU roles. Multi-agent pipeline (DeepSeek, Claude, OpenAI) for automated classification, skill extraction, and natural-language search. Next.js 16, GraphQL, Cloudflare D1, ingestion from 3 ATS platforms, production LLM observability.
highlights: Built full-stack AI job board: Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, TypeScript 5.9, GraphQL API layer, automated data pipelines, multi-model LLM orchestration, edge-deployed serverless Cloudflare Workers + D1 cloud infrastructure.

Architected multi-LLM routing layer — Claude for high-accuracy classification, DeepSeek for cost-efficient bulk processing — backed by Promptfoo + Mastra Evals for accuracy validation and zero-downtime model swaps.

Engineered agentic AI pipeline using Mastra agents, LangGraph, and Vercel AI SDK v6 orchestrating DeepSeek, Claude, and OpenAI for real-time classification, skill extraction, and EU remote categorization.

Implemented event-driven data ingestion from 3 ATS platforms (Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby) via Trigger.dev + Inngest orchestration with retry policies, dead-letter queues, Brave Search API discovery, and CI/CD-integrated pipeline validation.

Built RAG-powered hybrid search combining BM25 lexical scoring with vector embeddings (LlamaIndex/LlamaParse), plus text-to-SQL converting natural language queries into parameterized Drizzle ORM expressions against D1.

Designed GraphQL API with DataLoader-based batching to eliminate N+1 queries on job-to-company resolution; Drizzle ORM + GraphQL Codegen for end-to-end TypeScript type safety from DB schema to Apollo Client hooks.
Engineered Cloudflare D1 Gateway Worker achieving 10-100x throughput improvement over REST API via batch query support, stale-while-revalidate caching, and structured error handling.
Developed Python Worker on Cloudflare for LangChain LCEL chain execution with Pydantic model validation and schema enforcement, enabling zero-cost LLM inference via Workers AI.

aiProjects.name: ResearchThera.com
aiProjects.description: AI-powered therapeutic journaling platform with agentic orchestration (Mastra + DeepSeek), 7-source academic research pipeline with AI quality gating, clinical NLP normalization, RAG retrieval, voice synthesis, and Langfuse prompt management. Next.js 16, GraphQL, Cloudflare D1/R2, Trigger.dev.
highlights: Built AI-powered therapeutic journaling platform with multi-agent Mastra orchestration (storyTellerAgent + therapeuticAgent powered by DeepSeek), Next.js 16, React 19, Apollo Server 5, Cloudflare D1/R2, and end-to-end type safety via GraphQL Codegen + Drizzle ORM.

Designed multi-stage agentic workflow: DeepSeek clinical NLP normalization → parallel 7-source research ingestion → OpenAlex enrichment (300 candidates) → LLM quality gate → top-20 persistence to D1 + vector store for RAG retrieval.

Built custom 'research-grounding' evaluation scorer using DeepSeek-as-judge for evidence validation; tuned 3-threshold gating (relevance 0.75, confidence 0.55, blended 0.72) to filter unsupported clinical claims.

Developed clinical NLP restatement system normalizing multilingual user goals into clinical language — classifying developmental tiers and behavioral direction to constrain outputs across CBT, MBSR, ACT, and DBT frameworks.

Implemented Langfuse prompt versioning with runtime registry loading for no-deploy prompt updates, request-level distributed tracing for latency attribution, per-model cost tracking, and A/B prompt evaluation.
Engineered serverless TTS microservice chunking content via RAG segmentation, streaming audio to R2 with presigned URL playback — decoupled via Trigger.dev v4 durable background tasks.
Architected schema-first GraphQL API with TypeScript type safety via GraphQL Codegen; WebSocket subscriptions for real-time progress tracking with long-running research and audio jobs.

volunteer.summary:
• Contributed 100+ pull requests to core trading engine and exchange connectivity modules in Rust and Python.
  • Built production exchange adapters for dYdX v4 and Hyperliquid with REST, WebSocket, gRPC, authentication, rate limiting, and execution clients.
  • Enhanced OKX and Kraken integrations with historical data support, pagination, and WebSocket reliability improvements.
  • Implemented high-performance technical indicators in Rust with Python/Cython parity, improving PnL calculations and monetary precision.
